label: Making A First Mod
title: Making a first mod
bodyHtml:
What are we building

This introductory guide will add one custom item called acidshroom, one custom liquid called hydrochloricacid, one buildingEntity called acidmushroom, and two simple recipes.

The code sample on the right is intentionally written as one compact registration flow so you can copy it into a first mod and then split it into files later.

Still, it's best to follow step-by-step rather then copy-paste.

Folder layout and assets

Create three embedded resources in your project: Images.acidshroom.png, Images.acidmushroom.png, and Audio.acid-pop.wav. Set each file's Build Action to Embedded Resource.

If you need a refresher on the loaders themselves, the dedicated Loading Assets and Audio pages go deeper on suffix matching, caching, and loose file loading.

// Examples used by this tutorial
Sprite acidShroomSprite = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.acidshroom.png");
Sprite acidMushroomSprite = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.acidmushroom.png", 8f);
AudioClip acidPop = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedAudio("Audio.acid-pop.wav");

Consider opengameart.org for free assets and sounds.

Register the item and liquid

The acidshroom item is a normal pickup item that can be eaten for a tiny benefit and a little risk. The custom liquid uses CustomLiquidInfo so it can later be put into containers or recipe inputs.

For more field-by-field references, see Item API, Advanced Item API, and Liquid API.

Register the building entity

The acidmushroom buildingEntity is a simple floor plant. It uses the embedded sprite, reuses the loaded sound as a custom hit sound, and drops the harvest item when destroyed.

If you want to expand this into a more interactive machine later, jump to Building Entities and Advanced Buildings.

Register the recipes

This walkthrough uses two recipes:

  • acidmushroom into 40 biochem.
  • 2 acidshroom plus processedcopper into one mediumbattery.

The dedicated Recipe API page explains the recipe object model in more depth, especially when you start mixing qualities, liquids, and condition values.

Where to go next

Once this works, the next useful additions are usually Locale for explicit translations, Console for spawn/debug commands, and more content.

The tutorial sample keeps everything in one place on purpose. When you are ready, split it into RegisterItems.cs, RegisterLiquids.cs, RegisterBuildings.cs, and RegisterRecipes.cs.
